ingredients: 4 fillets sole or plaice, skin-on (about 140 g each)
Salt and black pepper, to taste
3 tbsp light olive oil (or sunflower oil)
steps: Season flour with salt and pepper. Coat fish in flour and shake off excess.
Heat oil in a large frying pan. Cook fish skin-side down about 2 minutes, then flip and cook 1 to 2 minutes until golden and just cooked.
Remove fish to a warm plate and season. Wipe out the pan, then melt butter until it turns light brown.
Stir in lemon juice and capers (optional). Return fish briefly and spoon sauce over.
tips: Dry the fish well before flouring for the best browning; do not overcook thin fillets.
